title: Zero to Hero AI — The Complete ML Knowledge Architecture
description: A comprehensive, pedagogically-ordered curriculum covering Python, Mathematics, ML, Deep Learning, NLP, LLMs, and Production Systems. Built for absolute beginners progressing to expert level.
learning_order_validation: Validated via web search: Math → Python → ML → DL → NLP → LLMs → Production (standard curriculum order). Authors' content ordering cross-referenced across 5 source books.
source_books: Stage 1: Math & Foundations Master (1539 pages, 17 chapters)
Stage 2: Core ML Master (2695 pages, 18 chapters)
Stage 3: Deep Learning Master (2126 pages, 15 chapters)
Stage 4: NLP & LLMs Master (1076 pages, 19 chapters)
Stage 5: ML Systems & Production Master (3223 pages, 24 chapters)

children.title: Why Python for Data Science and AI
IDEs and Text Editors (VS Code, PyCharm)
String Methods (upper, lower, strip, split, join, replace, find)
List Methods (append, extend, insert, remove, pop, sort, reverse)
When to Use Tuples vs Lists
Dictionary Methods (keys, values, items, get, update, pop)

Basic Syntax [expr for item in iterable]
Conditional Filtering [expr for item in iterable if condition]

File Modes (r, w, a, rb, wb)

pip install, pip freeze, pip list

Models with High Bias (Linear Regression on Non-linear Data)
Symptoms of High Bias (Low Training Accuracy)
Models with High Variance (Deep Trees, High-Degree Polynomials)

Total Error = Bias² + Variance + Irreducible Error

Strategies to Reduce Variance (More Data, Regularization, Simpler Models)
Causes (Too Complex Model, Too Little Data, Noise)

Objective: Minimize Within-Cluster Sum of Squares
